summary: reconcileStreetsAction updates/inserts route_street positions in walking order against the new non-deferrable UNIQUE (route_id, position) constraint, so any street-list edit that isn't a pure append (reorder, whole-route reverse, mid-route removal, prepend) hits a transient position collision and the PUT 500s.
failure_scenario: REPRODUCED LIVE: saved route [street 1970] as route 9, then PUT /userapi/routes/9 with streets [28, 1970-reversed] → 500 with PSQLException 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint route_street_route_id_position_key, Key (route_id, position)=(9, 0) already exists'. Removed rows are deleted only after the updates, so mid-route removal collides the same way. withSlugRetry retries the identical transaction and fails again.

summary: The PR deletes public/images/icons/routebuilder/start-point.png but public/css/admin-task.css .user-marker still uses it as its background-image, so the admin task-playback page loses its user-position marker.
failure_scenario: Admin opens /admin/task/<id>; .user-marker renders as an empty 27x27 box because /assets/images/icons/routebuilder/start-point.png now 404s — the playback position on the map is invisible.

summary: saveRoute validates name/description but not that streets is non-empty (updateRoute does reject an empty list), and the new route-scoped mission path crashes Explore for a zero-street route.
failure_scenario: POST /saveRoute with {"region_id": N, "streets": []} returns 200 and creates a route with no route_street rows; /explore?routeId=<id> then hits getRouteDistance == 0 → mission DBIO yields None → ExploreService.scala:250 .map(_.get) throws → 500. The route is also invisible in /userapi/routes and the dashboard (inner join on route_street), so the owner can't delete it from the UI.
